values: A trader from the other settlement left with more than they paid for
Someone helped themselves to it during the last market day
It went missing right after those merchants passed through
I know exactly who took it. They're not even trying to hide it
It was on the table one moment and gone the next. I have a good idea who
A neighbor borrowed it and never gave it back. We both know that's not borrowing
They walked out with it tucked under their arm like it was theirs
I trusted the wrong person with access to it. Simple as that
Somebody swapped it for a cheaper version and thought I wouldn't notice
The woman who took it probably thinks she's clever. She's not
The peddler who came through last week left lighter than he arrived. So did I
One of the farmhands pocketed it during the harvest feast and I can prove it
I saw it in someone else's hands at the market. My initials are still on it
They'll say they found it. They didn't find it. They took it from my workshop
A deal went sour and the other party decided to keep what wasn't theirs
It vanished the same day a certain family moved into the settlement. Coincidence
The apprentice who left my service took a few things that weren't part of the arrangement
Someone at the crossroads inn has it. I recognized it through the window
A courier was supposed to deliver it. The courier arrived. The item didn't
I've been told it's sitting in plain sight in someone's workshop. Brazen
A rival crafter took it to study. Studying doesn't usually last six months
The person who has it knows it's not theirs. They just don't care
It was collateral for a debt that was paid in full. They kept it anyway
Somebody lifted it during the commotion at the last gathering
I lent it in good faith and was told it was lost. It wasn't lost
A relative took it when they visited. Family makes the worst thieves
It was in the cart when I left. It wasn't in the cart when I arrived
The smith's apprentice has sticky fingers. Everyone knows it, nobody says it
Someone decided my loss was their gain. I'd like to correct that
I wouldn't call it stolen. I'd call it taken without asking and never returned
